title: Images of the southern Chile subduction zone from full-waveform inversion
journal: Journal of Geophysical Research: Solid Earth
abstract: Full-waveform inversion (FWI) is applied to the MGL1701 CEVICHE long-offset seismic dataset along the south-central Chilean margin (36°–41°S) to derive high-resolution P-wave velocity models. The FWI results image underplating of oceanic material beneath the continental wedge and constrain the deep structure of the subduction zone. Velocity anomalies are correlated with the extent of the 2010 Mw 8.8 Maule earthquake rupture.

location.description: South-central Chilean subduction zone 36°–41°S; full-waveform inversion of MGL1701 CEVICHE long-offset MCS data imaging underplating

seismic_lines.name: MGL1701 CEVICHE Lines — South-Central Chile 36°–41°S (FWI imaging)

data.name: MGL1701 CEVICHE Long-Offset MCS Data — South-Central Chile 36°–41°S (FWI)
data.description: MGL1701 CEVICHE 15.15-km streamer long-offset MCS data processed with full-waveform inversion (FWI) to image underplating and high-resolution velocity structure of the south-central Chilean subduction zone (36°–41°S)

_data_note: JGR paper; behind paywall. MGL1701 CEVICHE data at MGDS (open access). Companion to Bangs et al. 2020 and Maksymowicz et al. 2021.

processing.summary: Full-waveform inversion (FWI) applied to MGL1701 CEVICHE long-offset (15.15 km) seismic data along 36°–41°S provides high-resolution P-wave velocity models. Results image underplating of oceanic material beneath the continental wedge and constrain the deep subduction zone structure correlated with the 2010 Maule earthquake rupture extent.
processing.notes: JGR 2022 paper using MGL1701 CEVICHE data with FWI; companion to Bangs et al. 2020 and Maksymowicz et al. 2021

analysis_notes: JGR paper; behind paywall. MGL1701 CEVICHE data at MGDS (open access). High confidence from CEVICHE cruise knowledge.
